sections.heading: These Regulations are the Cybersecurity (Provider-Owned Critical Information
sections.summary: This section establishes the title and effective date of the Cybersecurity (Provider-Owned Critical Information Infrastructure) Regulations 2018, which came into force on 31 August 2018. It specifies that these regulations are part of Singapore's legal framework for critical information infrastructure security. The section does not impose any obligations or conditions but provides foundational context for the regulatory framework.

requirements.text: These Regulations are the Cybersecurity (Provider-Owned Critical Information Infrastructure) Regulations 2018 and come into operation on 31 August 2018.

sections.source_quote: These Regulations are the Cybersecurity (Provider-Owned Critical Information Infrastructure) Regulations 2018 and come into operation on 31 August 2018.

sections.heading: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires —
sections.summary: This section defines key terms used throughout the Cybersecurity (Provider-Owned Critical Information) Regulations, including 'owner-controlled interconnected computer or computer system', 'owner-controlled non-interconnected computer or computer system', 'relevant computer or computer system', and 'supplier-controlled interconnected computer or computer system'. It also defines 'working day' as any day except Saturday, Sunday, or public holiday. These definitions establish the technical and operational scope for critical information infrastructure in Singapore. This section mandates specific reporting obligations for cybersecurity incidents involving provider-owned critical information infrastructure in Singapore. Owners must submit detailed incident reports to the Commissioner within 2 hours of certain critical circumstances, provide supplementary details within 72 hours, and submit a final incident report within 30 days. The reporting must include incident details, impact on systems, business operations, and remedial measures. The Commissioner specifies the format and submission methods, including telephone, text message, or written reports via the official government website. T
sections.dtia_summary: These definitions clarify the technical scope of critical information infrastructure and related systems, which is essential for determining data transfer and processing contexts in cross-border scenarios involving Singapore's critical infrastructure. This section establishes time-bound reporting requirements for cybersecurity incidents that affect critical infrastructure, directly impacting data transfer impact assessments by requiring detailed incident documentation and timely notifications to regulators for cross-border data security incidents involving critical systems.

requirements.text: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires —

"owner-controlled interconnected computer or computer system" means any computer or computer system under the control of the owner of a provider-owned critical information infrastructure, that is interconnected with or that communicates with the provider-owned critical information infrastructure

"owner-controlled non-interconnected computer or computer system" means any computer or computer system under the control of the owner of a provider-owned critical information infrastructure, that is not the provider-owned critical information infrastructure or an owner-controlled interconnected computer or computer system

"relevant computer or computer system" means — (a) a provider-owned critical information infrastructure; (b) an owner-controlled interconnected computer or computer system; (c) an owner-controlled non-interconnected computer or computer system; or (d) a supplier-controlled interconnected computer or computer system

"supplier-controlled interconnected computer or computer system" means any computer or computer system under the control of a supplier to the owner of a provider-owned critical information infrastructure, that is interconnected with or that communicates with the provider-owned critical information infrastructure
"working day" means any day except a Saturday, Sunday or public holiday

The owner must submit details of the cybersecurity incident within 2 hours after the occurrence of any circumstance mentioned in paragraph (2D) via telephone, text message, or written report on the government website.
The owner must provide supplementary details within 72 hours after the occurrence of the circumstance, to the fullest extent practicable, in the form specified on the government website.
The owner must submit a final incident report within 30 days (including Sundays and public holidays) after the initial submission, containing updated details and supplementary information.
The final incident report must include the details submitted under paragraphs (1)(a) and (2C), as well as any updates and supplementary details.
The owner must notify the Commissioner of the cybersecurity incident within 30 days after becoming aware of it, in a consolidated quarterly report.

The owner must provide the date and time of the incident, the affected critical information infrastructure, the owner's name and contact number, the computer system involved, the nature of the incident, its cause, resulting effects, and remedial measures.

In this regulation, 'cybersecurity risk', in relation to a provider-owned critical information infrastructure, means the risk that a vulnerability in the cybersecurity of the provider-owned critical information infrastructure may be exploited by a cybersecurity threat or incident.

sections.source_quote: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires — "owner-controlled interconnected computer or computer system" means any computer or computer system under the control of the owner of a provider-owned critical information infrastructure, that is interconnected with or that communicates with the provider-owned critical information infrastructure; [S 678/2025 wef 31/10/2025]
